description_long: This object consists of a small group of old, metal folding chairs, some upright and some collapsed and lying on the ground. The chairs have a utilitarian design, with thin tubular metal frames and flat, rectangular seats and backrests, all showing significant wear and rust. The surfaces are covered in a dull, grayish finish with visible stains, scratches, and patches of corrosion, indicating prolonged outdoor or heavy use. The seats and backs are made from a thin sheet of metal, slightly bent and warped in places. The legs are slender and straight, with simple rubber or plastic feet, some of which appear to be missing or worn down. The overall appearance suggests these chairs are often found in urban environments such as parks, garages, or event spaces where durability is prioritized over comfort.
description: A group of old, weathered metal folding chairs, some upright and some collapsed, with rusted frames and worn gray surfaces.
description_view_0: Two upright folding chairs and two collapsed chairs are visible, showing the worn metal surfaces and utilitarian design.
description_view_1: The view highlights the backs of the upright chairs and the flat, collapsed seats lying on the ground.
description_view_2: This angle shows the side profiles of the upright chairs and the collapsed chairs, emphasizing the thin metal frames.
description_view_3: The backs and seats of the upright chairs are prominent, with the collapsed chairs partially obscured between them.
